summary: Game-cheat tool with stealth Windows persistence, process masquerading, and eval of obfuscated payload
details: On load (main=launcher.js), the package establishes hidden persistence on Windows by writing an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' via PowerShell (launcher.js:14), pointing at a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs that silently re-launches `node launcher.js` at every user login using `objShell.Run "node.exe launcher.js", 0, False` (hidden window). The registry key and VBS filename impersonate Microsoft OneDrive to evade inspection. launcher.js also sets `process.title = 'Runtime Broker'` to masquerade as the legitimate Windows RuntimeBroker.exe in Task Manager, and installs no-op `uncaughtException` / `unhandledRejection` handlers to keep the disguised process from crashing visibly. At load time launcher.js reads sibling file server.obf.js (an obfuscator.io-style RC4-packed bundle ~80KB) and passes its entire contents to `eval()`; the decoded payload performs license HTTP calls, opens a socket.io control channel, builds an HWID from registry MachineGuid + hostname + volume serial, and dispatches commands to a bundled 6.8MB native Windows addon (sens.node). The native module and public/index.html advertise game-cheat features (Aimbot/Triggerbot/ESP/Teleport/Vehicle-Spawn for a game called 'Forte'), and sens.node contains bone-name strings ('Head','LeftFoot','LeftHand') consistent with an aimbot. Installer harm: any developer who pulls this package (directly or via dependency confusion) gets persistent stealth software auto-launched at every login under a Microsoft-impersonating registry entry, with an obfuscated remote-controlled native module.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
